options.a: supercooled water droplets form small snow crystals and reduce visibility.
options.b: tiny water droplets in the air remain as liquid in temperatures below freezing and reduce visibility.
options.c: tiny water droplets in the air change into clear ice as soon as the air temperature falls below 0°C
options.d: saturated air with water droplets is cooled down below 0°C and small ice crystals reduce visibility.
